title: A Sand County Almanac
excerpt: The founding text of modern conservation ethics - a lyrical month-by-month account of life on a Wisconsin farm that builds to a revolutionary philosophy of land stewardship.
keyIdeas: A land ethic enlarges the boundaries of community to include soils, waters, plants, and animals
Conservation is a state of harmony between men and land
Thinking like a mountain means understanding the ecological role of predators
The value of wilderness cannot be reduced to economic terms
keyTakeaways: A thing is right when it tends to preserve the integrity, stability, and beauty of the biotic community
The land is not a commodity to be owned but a community to which we belong
Ecological education must engage the heart as well as the mind
Wilderness is not a luxury but a necessity for human flourishing
whoShouldRead: Hunters, farmers, and anyone who works the land
Students of environmental ethics and philosophy
Readers of Thoreau, Muir, and Carson
whoShouldSkip: Those seeking a scientific ecology textbook
Readers uncomfortable with hunting as a conservation practice
